adds.rationale: TSMC is the sole-source fab behind every leading-edge AI accelerator; Q1 FY25 Nvidia data center revenue at $22.6B (+427% YoY) and reports that NVDA/AMD/AVGO have locked up TSMC's 3nm capacity make TSM the picks-and-shovels exposure missing from the current AI cluster.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia reports record Q1 FY25 data center revenue of $22.6B, +427% YoY; announces 10-for-1 split effective June 7, 2024 — direct read-through to TSMC fab demand.
TSMC board approves ~$17.4B in additional capex appropriations for advanced node and packaging capacity to meet long-term demand forecasts.
TSMC June 2024 net revenue NT$207.87B, +32.9% YoY; H1 2024 revenue up 28% YoY — AI-driven advanced-node demand visible in monthly run-rate.

removes.rationale: URA's top holding is CCJ (~22% weight), which we already hold directly; dropping the ETF de-duplicates uranium exposure without changing the bucket thesis and frees a slot for a higher-conviction AI-infrastructure name.

news_evidence.summary: Global X URA top holdings disclosure shows Cameco as the single largest constituent, with significant overlap to a direct CCJ position; uranium thesis remains intact via CCJ alone.
news_evidence.source: Global X URA fund page (holdings as of 2024-Q2)

rationale_overall: Single swap this quarter: add TSM (AI fab bottleneck — direct beneficiary of the Nvidia data-center surge visible in May 22 earnings and TSMC's June 5 capex bump), drop URA (uranium ETF whose largest holding is CCJ, which we already own — de-duplication). Net effect: sharpens AI-infrastructure depth from chip-designer (NVDA) to chip-fab (TSM) while keeping the nuclear bucket intact via CCJ. Watchlist size stays at 16, all other thematic buckets (rockets, robotics, quantum, geopolitical-energy, demographics, defense) unchanged.
